children.text: in a manner that conserves, protects, and enhances the purposes for which the Recreation Area is established; and

children.label: A

children.label: C
children.text: other applicable laws.

children.heading: Uses
children.label: b Uses
children.text: The Secretary shall allow only uses of the Recreation Area that are consistent with the purposes for which the Recreation Area is established.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 5 years after , the Secretary shall develop a comprehensive management plan for the long-term protection and management of the Recreation Area.March 12, 2019

children.label: A
children.text: describe the appropriate uses and management of the Recreation Area;

children.label: B
children.text: be developed with extensive public input;

children.label: C
children.text: take into consideration any information developed in studies of the land within the Recreation Area; and

children.label: D
children.text: be developed fully consistent with the settlement agreement entered into on , in the case in the United States District Court for the District of Utah styled “Southern Utah Wilderness Alliance, et al. v. U.S. Department of the Interior, et al.” and numbered 2:12–cv–257 DAK.January 13, 2017

children.heading: Motorized vehicles
children.label: 1 Motorized vehicles
children.text: Except as needed for emergency response or administrative purposes, the use of motorized vehicles in the Recreation Area shall be permitted only on roads and motorized routes designated in the Management Plan for the use of motorized vehicles.

children.heading: New roads
children.label: 2 New roads
children.text: No new permanent or temporary roads or other motorized vehicle routes shall be constructed within the Recreation Area after .March 12, 2019

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Necessary maintenance or repairs to existing roads designated in the Management Plan for the use of motorized vehicles, including necessary repairs to keep existing roads free of debris or other safety hazards, shall be permitted after , consistent with the requirements of this section.March 12, 2019

children.heading: Effect
children.label: B Effect
children.text: Nothing in this subsection prevents the Secretary from rerouting an existing road or trail to protect Recreation Area resources from degradation or to protect public safety, as determined to be appropriate by the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: applicable law (including regulations); and

children.label: B
children.text: the purposes of the Recreation Area.

children.heading: Inventory
children.label: 2 Inventory
children.text: Not later than 5 years after , the Secretary, in collaboration with any affected grazing permittee, shall carry out an inventory of facilities and improvements associated with grazing activities in the Recreation Area.March 12, 2019

children.heading: Cold War sites
children.label: f Cold War sites
children.text: The Secretary shall manage the Recreation Area in a manner that educates the public about Cold War and historic uranium mine sites in the Recreation Area, subject to such terms and conditions as the Secretary considers necessary to protect public health and safety.

children.heading: Incorporation of acquired land and interests
children.label: g Incorporation of acquired land and interests

children.text: become part of the Recreation Area; and
be managed in accordance with applicable laws, including as provided in this section.
entry, appropriation, or disposal under the public land laws;
location, entry, and patent under the mining laws; and
operation of the mineral leasing, mineral materials, and geothermal leasing laws.

children.heading: Study of nonmotorized recreation opportunities
children.label: i Study of nonmotorized recreation opportunities
children.text: Not later than 2 years after , the Secretary, in consultation with interested parties, shall conduct a study of nonmotorized recreation trail opportunities, including bicycle trails, within the Recreation Area, consistent with the purposes of the Recreation Area.March 12, 2019

children.heading: Cooperative agreement
children.label: j Cooperative agreement
children.text: The Secretary may enter into a cooperative agreement with the State in accordance with section 307(b) of the Federal Land Policy and Management Act of 1976 () and other applicable laws to provide for the protection, management, and maintenance of the Recreation Area.43 U.S.C. 1737(b)
